sast_review.reason: pickle.loads on attacker-controlled base64 data allows arbitrary code execution. The code does not implement the scenario's requirement to whitelist only primitive types (strings, integers, lists, dictionaries).
ElementTree.fromstring without defusedxml is vulnerable to XML bombs and entity expansion attacks. No external entity protection is implemented.
Importing pickle is a risk when used on untrusted data, which it is on line 9. The import enables the unsafe deserialization.
Importing ElementTree without defusedxml is a risk when parsing untrusted XML, which happens on line 19.

raw.summary: Critical security issues: unsafe pickle deserialization (no primitive type whitelist), XML parsing without entity protection, SQL injection via undefined `sql` variable (NameError), missing filters handling, missing HTML escaping, layout validation incorrect (doesn't allow spaces/directives), code does not match functional requirements (returns dict instead of HTTP response, missing expr evaluation, missing HTML rendering). All SAST findings are true positives.
